# Task Log: Create Text-Based Game UI
**Date:** 12-20-2025 11:43  
**Project:** Ubiquitous  
**Task:** Create a simple user interface in the Ubiquitous WPF project to play text-based games

## Objective
Implement a WPF UI that allows users to play text-based adventure games using the existing TextGame library.

## Analysis
- Reviewed existing TextGame library structure:
  - Game.cs: Main game controller with console-based input/output
  - CommandParser.cs: Parses natural language commands
  - Room.cs: Represents locations
  - Item.cs: Represents items (needs to be reviewed)
  - Player.cs: Player state and inventory
- Missing classes referenced in Game.cs:
  - World class
  - Inventory class (referenced in Player)
- MainWindow.xaml is currently empty

## Implementation Plan
1. Create missing support classes (World, Inventory)
2. Modify Game.cs to support UI-based interaction (decouple from Console)
3. Design and implement WPF UI in MainWindow.xaml:
   - Text output area for game narrative
   - Command input field
   - Command history
   - Inventory display
4. Implement MainWindow.xaml.cs logic to interface with Game engine
5. Create a sample game for testing

## Changes Made
